content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

August 15, 1991

David R. Kruss  
312 Menhaden Court  
Foster City, CA 94404-1933

Re: Your Request For Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-91-367

Dear Mr. Kruss:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

Your letter, dated July 25, 1991, correctly summarizes my advice that there is no provision under the Act which prohibits the use of the word "re-elect" on your campaign signs and in your campaign advertising materials. A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

Please note that the Commission's jurisdiction extends only to the Act. The Commission has no jurisdiction to advise on other provisions of the law which may apply to your situation. You should check with your district attorney or the Attorney General's office to determine if another provision of the law may apply.

Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Alice Hughes  
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
